modules.name: DigiGraph
modules.tagline: One supervisor decides which specialist runs. Every time.
summary: Single-agent setups break the moment a request needs more than one tool path. DigiGraph's LangGraph supervisor inspects each request and routes it to the right sub-graph — quant research, vector retrieval, chat — via a declarative tool registry any new vertical can plug into.

The front door speaks the OpenAI API, so existing clients work unchanged. LiteLLM handles model routing and caching; LangGraph checkpoints persist state across hops, so a multi-step run survives a restart.

modules.name: DigiQuant
modules.tagline: Strategy research that ends in an order, not a markdown file.
summary: Atlas runs scheduled research cycles, Hermes turns the output into signals, Kairos executes them on a NautilusTrader core. Every step writes to an immutable audit trail; live trading stays loopback-only until a human flips the gate.
No black-box cloud platform, no vendor-priced data feed required — backtest, optimize, and deploy on infrastructure you own.

modules.name: DigiSearch
modules.tagline: Production RAG without a stack rewrite when you switch vector DB.
summary: One DigiSearch client over Chroma, pgvector, Qdrant, or in-memory — backend-neutral Document, Chunk, Query, and Result entities mean you swap engines without touching business code.
Dense, sparse, and hybrid retrieval are first-class — pick per query, not per index. Polars throughout: ingest pipelines stay fast on a laptop and scale to a server without rewriting in pandas.

modules.name: DigiChat
modules.tagline: Talk to your stack with your keys, your models, your audit log.
summary: BYOK, every request: paste your Anthropic, OpenAI, or LiteLLM-compatible key and DigiChat forwards it to DigiGraph per-request — never stored, never logged.
The same Next.js BFF brokers machine API keys for service-to-service calls, so a single deployment serves humans and agents.

modules.name: DigiKey
modules.role: Auth · RS256 JWTs · scoped API keys
modules.tagline: Identity, JWTs, and scoped keys — one issuer for humans and machines.
summary: RS256-signed JWTs with a published JWKS, org + project membership, and row-level resource scopes baked into the token. Issues scoped API keys for service-to-service calls.
SSO identity federation so the same identity flows across every module.

modules.name: DigiSmith
modules.tagline: Correlation IDs across every span; PII redacted before logs hit disk.
summary: Structured logging, Prometheus metrics, and correlation IDs threaded through every request so a multi-hop run is traceable end to end.
PII is redacted before anything is written, so your observability posture is compliant out of the box.

modules.name: DigiClaw
modules.tagline: The always-on agent runtime — scheduling, heartbeats, immutable audit.
summary: An OpenClaw-based orchestration layer that keeps agents running: Atlas runner scheduling, heartbeats, and drift detection.
Every action lands in an immutable audit log and is exposed over MCP.

modules.name: DigiBase
modules.tagline: The shared Python library every module builds on — and nothing more.
summary: Not a service — a deliberately minimal library of shared HTTP and audit primitives so every module behaves consistently.
Audit redaction, request helpers, and common types live here once.

modules.name: DigiStore
modules.tagline: One storage API over S3, MinIO, Postgres, or SQLite.
summary: A storage abstraction layer so business code never binds to a backend: configure S3, MinIO, Postgres, or SQLite and the API stays the same.
Run SQLite on a laptop, swap to S3 + Postgres in production without rewriting.

modules.name: DigiLink
modules.tagline: A protocol bridge so non-native transports speak MCP.
summary: Register adapters that translate REST, gRPC, or bespoke transports into MCP tools, so anything can plug into the orchestrator.
The translation layer that keeps the stack open instead of locked to one protocol.
